cases.input: autobahn invoked after a northstar handoff with three sliced goals is present in .ai/.
cases.expected_behavior: Discovers the handoff, then ships each sliced goal as exactly one PR via ultragoal. Runs the architect+reviewer+executor peer-review loop, resolves all comments, and requires remote AND local CI green before any merge decision.

cases.input: autobahn must select an execution engine for a qa-heavy goal, a parallelizable goal, and a persistence-heavy goal, with no --engine override.
cases.expected_behavior: Applies the deterministic precedence ultraqa (qa-heavy) > ultrawork (parallelizable) > ralph (persistence) > team (default fallback). With --engine set, honors the override. An unrecognized engine exits non-zero rather than silently defaulting.

cases.input: autobahn reaches the merge step with a host-policy verdict that is NOT approved (or carries no valid confirmation token).
cases.expected_behavior: The merge-authority adapter consumes the host-policy verdict verbatim and does not re-derive the token regex or admin rule. Merges only on an approved verdict WITH a valid token; on a blocked/default verdict it stops at ready-for-human; on a rejected verdict it fails closed. Never self-approves.

cases.input: autobahn closes the originating issue after a goal merges, and the closure step runs twice.
cases.expected_behavior: Delegates closure to the cascade engine with the canonical triage status. The second close resolves by the stable idempotency key, creates no duplicate, and still appends an audit event. Closure never invents a status string.

cases.input: autobahn is invoked without a northstar handoff but with one goal record whose context, root causes, evidence, solution, acceptance criteria, scope, verification, issue reference, and coverage are complete.
cases.expected_behavior: Runs the direct readiness gate, creates one durable goal, and proceeds through the normal TDD, engine, review, CI, merge-authority, and closure gates. If any readiness evidence is missing or contradictory, it stops and routes to northstar or diagnose rather than discovering during implementation.

cases.input: autobahn receives one goal with 18% relevant coverage, and another with 72% coverage plus agent-recorded high coupling at the change seam.
cases.expected_behavior: Selects legacy-safe TDD for both goals: automatically for coverage under 30%, and by the recorded contextual risk override at 72%. It characterizes only one change seam, writes a failing behavior test, sprouts new behavior behind a narrow interface, makes the minimum call-site edit, and avoids broad refactoring or a coverage campaign.
